---
title: "Investing in Artificial General Intelligence"
date: today
date-format: long
format:
  html:
    output-file: ai_lab_investment.html
  pdf:
    output-file: ai_lab_investment.pdf
authors:
  - name: Vincent Grégoire
    affil-id: 1
    affiliation:
      - ref: 1
affiliations:
  - id: 1
    name: HEC Montréal
abstract: |
  Frontier AI laboratories face uncertain timing of transformative demand and must allocate scarce compute between current monetization (inference) and future capability-building (training).
  I develop a real-options model of irreversible capacity investment with regime switching, duopoly competition via Tullock contests, endogenous default risk, and diminishing returns calibrated to AI scaling laws.
  The model delivers analytical investment triggers and capacity for a single firm, and semi-analytical characterization of a duopoly preemption equilibrium in which the leader trains more and invests earlier than the follower.
  A "faith-based survival" mechanism emerges: training investment raises the effective revenue coefficient through the expected post-switch continuation value, lowering the default boundary---the hope of transformative AI keeps the firm alive.
  An asymmetric "Dario's dilemma" arises in which aggressive overinvestment carries higher downside risk than conservative underinvestment.
  A stylized calibration to four AI firm archetypes illustrates the magnitudes and highlights how heterogeneity in beliefs about AI timelines drives cross-sectional variation in investment behavior.
keywords:
  - real options
  - AI infrastructure
  - regime switching
  - training-inference allocation
  - duopoly preemption
  - default risk
thanks: "I thank Charles Martineau for helpful comments. Vincent Grégoire: HEC Montréal (vincent.gregoire@hec.ca)."
---


{{< include _introduction.qmd >}}

{{< include _model.qmd >}}

{{< include _calibration.qmd >}}

{{< include _valuation.qmd >}}

{{< include _discussion.qmd >}}

{{< include _conclusion.qmd >}}

\newpage
\clearpage

{{< include _appendix.qmd >}}

\newpage
\clearpage

## References {.unnumbered}

\begingroup
\raggedright
::: {#refs}
:::
\endgroup
